skills.name: Herbalism
skills.description:
Herbalism is the study and practical mastery of natural growths and botanical materials. It governs the identification, acquisition, and preparation of plants, fungi, and other naturally occurring organic substances used in crafting.

It encompasses three primary areas:

  • Identification: Recognizing natural growths and understanding their properties; distinguishing useful, edible, medicinal, toxic, or reactive specimens; identifying which parts hold potency; assessing freshness and quality; and spotting contamination, infestation, or natural mutation.
  • Gathering & Cultivation: Knowing where, when, and how to safely acquire botanical materials; harvesting without diminishing potency; extracting delicate materials such as sap, pollen, spores, or resin; preserving materials during transport; and cultivating or propagating useful plants.
  • Botanical Preparation: Transforming raw materials into usable forms through cleaning, trimming, drying, curing, and grinding; preparing teas, poultices, salves, oils, and basic infusions; reducing natural toxicity or stabilizing volatile properties; and readying botanical ingredients for broader crafting processes.

Herbalism focuses on natural materials and their direct preparation. It does not cover mineral refinement, advanced alchemical synthesis, or arcane essence manipulation.

perks.name: Field Forager
perks.description:
About the Perk
You understand how to read the land. You know where plants prefer to grow, when they are at peak potency, and how to harvest without spoiling their strength. You are no longer guessing, you are gathering with intent.

benefits.title: Recipe access
benefits.description: Unlocks recipe tiers 0 to 1.

benefits.title: Common Components
benefits.description: Unlocks components with skill requirements 0 to 3.

perks.name: Wildharvester
perks.description:
About the Perk
You have moved beyond simple collection. You recognize microclimates, subtle soil changes, and hidden growth patterns. Where others see forest, you see opportunity.

benefits.title: Gathering Bonus
benefits.description: You get a +2 bonus to your gathering rolls.

benefits.title: Yield Goodness
benefits.description: If you successfully gather, you gather 2 bundles instead of 1.

benefits.title: Uncommon Components
benefits.description: Unlocks components with skill requirements 4 to 9.

perks.name: Verdant Master
perks.description:
About the Perk
The wild yields to you. You harvest efficiently, cleanly, and at peak potency. Your hands know exactly when to cut, when to uproot, and when to leave growth undisturbed.

benefits.title: Gathering bonus
benefits.description: +4 bonus to Herbalism gathering rolls.

benefits.title: Double yield
benefits.description: Double yield on successful harvests.

benefits.title: Rare Components
benefits.description: Unlocks components with skill requirements 10 to 14.

perks.name: Seasoned Pathfinder
perks.description:
About the Perk
You do not wander blindly. You study soil, wind, moisture, and seasonal shifts. Where others see wilderness, you see patterns of growth and hidden opportunity.

benefits.title: Very Rare and Legendary Components
benefits.description: Unlocks components with skill requirements 15 to 20.

perks.name: Gentle Hand of the Grove
perks.description:
About the Perk
Your harvesting is precise and deliberate. You preserve root systems, protect potency, and avoid wasting what the land provides.

benefits.title: Reduced Waste
benefits.description: You waste nothing. Even when gathering fails you bundles all the scraps into an Herb Bundle, which is all you need for minor herbalism recipes.

perks.name: Initiate of the Green Archive
perks.description:
About the Perk
You have studied beyond folk remedies. You understand preparation theory, preservation methods, and the deeper lessons hidden within traditional botanical texts.

benefits.title: Recipe access
benefits.description: Unlocks recipe tiers 0 to 7.

benefits.title: Improved reliability
benefits.description: +1 to crafting DC when crafting within tier.

perks.name: Keeper of Hidden Remedies
perks.description:
About the Perk
You now study the dangerous and the misunderstood. You understand why certain plants are avoided, and how to safely coax power from them.

benefits.title: Recipe access
benefits.description: Unlocks recipe tiers 8 to 14.

perks.name: Archdruid of the Verdant Codex
perks.description:
About the Perk
You no longer follow recipes. You interpret them. You understand the philosophy of growth, decay, and potency. Your preparations approach masterwork quality.

benefits.title: Recipe access
benefits.description: Unlocks recipe tiers 15 to 20.

perks.name: Experimental Botanist
perks.description:
About the Perk
You do not wait for perfect instruction. You test, infuse, adjust, and document. Some of your discoveries are breakthroughs. Others are cautionary tales.

benefits.title: Experimental crafting
benefits.description: May attempt any herbalis recipe... with a few catches.

benefits.title: Ingredient Wildcard
benefits.description: You can read the recipe, but one of the components shouldn't be there. Remove it. good luck.

benefits.title: Experimental Crafting Bonus
benefits.description: Sure one is missing, but you get a +3 bonus to your crafting roll.

perks.name: Conservator of the Green Codex
perks.description:
About the Perk
Your notes are meticulous and your process is measured. You preserve knowledge, reduce waste, and refine preparation until it is repeatable under real conditions.

benefits.title: Reduced DC
benefits.description: -1 crafting DC when within tier.

benefits.title: Preserved ingredients
benefits.description: On failure lose only half the ingredients.

skills.name: Alchemy
skills.description:
Alchemy is the disciplined art of transforming materials through chemical, reactive, and arcane processes. It governs refinement, distillation, synthesis, and controlled reaction. Alchemy is concerned not with gathering ingredients, but with what happens when materials are combined, altered, or destabilized.

It encompasses three primary areas:

  • Reaction & Synthesis: Combining components to produce new substances; managing heat, pressure, and catalysts; preventing runaway reactions; controlling volatility and instability; and balancing opposing properties. Alchemy governs the moment materials cease being themselves and become something new.
  • Refinement & Distillation: Extracting, concentrating, or purifying material properties; distilling essences from raw inputs; separating impurities; enhancing potency; stabilizing unstable compounds; and converting crude materials into refined reagents.
  • Volatility & Control: Managing dangerous or unstable processes; preventing explosions or toxic releases; neutralizing hazardous byproducts; safely containing magical or chemical reactions; and recovering partial failures.

Alchemy governs potions, refined poisons, reactive compounds, stabilized essences, and advanced synthesis. It does not cover natural gathering, botanical identification, or basic field preparation of materials.

perks.name: Basic Alchemy
perks.description: You have the Alchemist's Supplies and the desire to brew potions. Nothing can stop you now. You can learn and craft common herbal recipes levels 0-1.

benefits.title: Recipe access
benefits.description: Unlocks recipe tiers 0 to 1.

perks.name: Advanced Alchemy
perks.description: You have a firm grasp on alchemy. You can learn and craft common herbal recipes levels 2-7.

benefits.title: Recipe access
benefits.description: Unlocks recipe tiers 2 to 7.

perks.name: Expert Alchemy
perks.description: You have a firm grasp on alchemy. You can learn and craft common herbal recipes levels 8-14.

benefits.title: Recipe access
benefits.description: Unlocks recipe tiers 8 to 14.

benefits.title: Steady hands
benefits.description: On failed alchemy attempts, lose only half the ingredients.

perks.name: Alchemy Mystic
perks.description: You have a firm grasp on alchemy. You can learn and craft common herbal recipes levels 14-20.

benefits.title: Recipe access
benefits.description: Unlocks recipe tiers 15 to 20.

benefits.title: Refined process
benefits.description: -1 crafting DC when crafting within unlocked tier.

perks.name: Experimental Alchemist
perks.description: You are willing to test unstable formulas and make controlled guesses when standard instructions run out.

benefits.title: Experimental alchemy
benefits.description: May attempt alchemy recipes above your unlocked tier.

benefits.title: Experimental wildcard
benefits.description: Experimental attempts include one wrong ingredient to identify and remove.

benefits.title: Experimental control
benefits.description: +2 to experimental crafting roll total.

perks.name: Failsoft Catalyst
perks.description: You build your process around controlled failure states. When reactions break, they break slowly and salvageably.

benefits.title: Catalyst dampening
benefits.description: On failed alchemy attempts, lose only half the ingredients.

benefits.title: Stability under pressure
benefits.description: +1 to experimental crafting roll total.

benefits.title: Lucky salvage
benefits.description: On failed craft, 25% chance to salvage a random tier-0 potion.

perks.name: Efficient Recovery
perks.description: Your cleanup and extraction discipline lets you reclaim useful material from successful runs.

benefits.title: Recovered stock
benefits.description: On success, keep half the ingredients.

benefits.title: Optimized process
benefits.description: -1 crafting DC when crafting within unlocked tier.

perks.name: Temporal Shifting
perks.description: You bend process timing around critical moments, compressing stable stages into a fraction of the usual time.

benefits.title: Compressed workflow
benefits.description: Reduce required crafting time by 50%.

benefits.title: Precision timing
benefits.description: -1 crafting DC when crafting within unlocked tier.

perks.name: Similar Enough
perks.description: Your practical chemistry lets you identify close analogs and substitute one ingredient by matching traits.

benefits.title: Trait substitution
benefits.description: May substitute 1 missing ingredient with a trait-matching ingredient.

benefits.title: Adaptive control
benefits.description: +1 to experimental crafting roll total.

perks.name: Volatile Gambit
perks.description: You embrace high-risk synthesis: brilliance on a perfect cast, catastrophic backlash on a fumble.

benefits.title: Critical surge
benefits.description: Natural 20 is automatic success with double output.

benefits.title: Backfire clause
benefits.description: Natural 1 is automatic failure and your kit explodes, dealing 1d10 self-damage.

skills.name: Poisoncraft
skills.description:
Poisoncraft is the specialized discipline of creating, refining, and deploying toxic substances for harm or incapacitation. It governs the extraction, concentration, stabilization, and application of poisons and venoms. Where Alchemy transforms materials broadly, Poisoncraft focuses on lethality, precision, and controlled delivery.

It encompasses three primary areas:

  • Extraction & Concentration: Isolating venom from creature parts; extracting toxic compounds from plants and fungi; concentrating mild irritants into dangerous agents; and preserving potency during storage and transport.
  • Refinement & Stabilization: Enhancing toxicity; adjusting onset time and duration; reducing detectable color, scent, or taste; preventing accidental degradation; and minimizing risk of self-exposure.
  • Delivery & Application: Coating weapons; preparing contact, ingested, or inhaled toxins; introducing poisons into food or drink; designing delayed or conditional activation; and safely handling volatile agents.

Poisoncraft governs weaponized toxins, applied venoms, and controlled delivery systems. It does not cover general botanical gathering, broad chemical synthesis, or restorative preparations.

perks.name: Basic Toxinwork
perks.description: You have the Poisoner's Kit and the discipline to handle toxic compounds safely. You can craft basic poisons and venoms.

benefits.title: Recipe access
benefits.description: Unlocks poisoncraft recipe tiers 0 to 1.

benefits.title: Delivery access
benefits.description: You can prepare basic injury poisons.

perks.name: Venom Distiller
perks.description: You can refine unstable extracts into consistent toxins and keep their potency through preparation.

benefits.title: Recipe access
benefits.description: Unlocks poisoncraft recipe tiers 2 to 7.

benefits.title: Steady process
benefits.description: +1 to crafting roll quality via reduced effective DC.

perks.name: Black Vials
perks.description: You can seal, transport, and apply dangerous compounds with precision under field pressure.

benefits.title: Recipe access
benefits.description: Unlocks poisoncraft recipe tiers 8 to 14.

benefits.title: Controlled handling
benefits.description: On failed poisoncraft attempts, lose only half the ingredients.

perks.name: Master Toxicologist
perks.description: You understand toxicity, onset, and delivery deeply enough to produce master-grade venoms and toxins.

benefits.title: Recipe access
benefits.description: Unlocks poisoncraft recipe tiers 15 to 20.

benefits.title: Master control
benefits.description: -1 crafting DC when crafting within unlocked tier.

perks.name: Experimental Toxicologist
perks.description: You can attempt volatile formulas beyond your normal training and adapt when a recipe is wrong or incomplete.

benefits.title: Experimental poisoncraft
benefits.description: May attempt poisoncraft recipes above your unlocked tier.

benefits.title: Experimental wildcard
benefits.description: Experimental attempts include one wrong ingredient to identify and remove.

benefits.title: Experimental control
benefits.description: +2 to experimental crafting roll total.

perks.name: Toxic Yield
perks.description: Your filtration and extraction process squeezes additional usable toxin from successful batches.

benefits.title: Recovery on success
benefits.description: On success, keep half the ingredients.

benefits.title: Bonus output chance
benefits.description: 25% chance for +1 extra poison output on success.

perks.name: Potency Tuning
perks.description: You can tune concentration and suspend compounds to preserve stronger effects without destabilizing the brew.

benefits.title: Potency boost
benefits.description: Increase poison potency by +2.

benefits.title: Precision process
benefits.description: -1 crafting DC when crafting within unlocked tier.

perks.name: Quiet Delivery
perks.description: You specialize in subtle application methods and can adapt compounds to more delivery vectors.

benefits.title: Delivery access
benefits.description: Unlocks contact and ingested delivery preparation.

benefits.title: Flexible substitution
benefits.description: 1 trait-based ingredient substitution allowed.

perks.name: Failsafe Handling
perks.description: Your safety discipline prevents catastrophic self-exposure even when a brew destabilizes.

benefits.title: Reduced self-harm
benefits.description: Critical-fumble poison self-damage is reduced by 50%.

benefits.title: Loss control
benefits.description: On failed poisoncraft attempts, lose only half the ingredients.

perks.name: Volatile Gambit
perks.description: You accept dangerous variance for exceptional highs. Great success brings explosive yield, and failure bites back.

benefits.title: Critical crafting
benefits.description: Nat 20 auto-succeeds with double output; nat 1 auto-fails with self-damage.

benefits.title: High-risk potency
benefits.description: Increase poison potency by +2.

skills.name: Cooking
skills.description:
Cooking is the practical craft of preparing food and drink that is nourishing, safe, and enjoyable. It governs selecting ingredients, applying heat and technique, preserving food, and combining flavors to produce meals, rations, and specialty dishes.

It encompasses three primary areas:

  • Preparation & Technique: Cleaning, trimming, grinding, mixing, and portioning ingredients; using knives and basic tools safely; and applying methods such as roasting, boiling, frying, baking, fermenting, smoking, and drying.
  • Flavor & Quality: Balancing taste and texture; managing doneness; adjusting seasoning; and recognizing when ingredients are fresh, spoiled, or contaminated.
  • Preservation & Provisioning: Producing travel-ready rations; preserving food through salting, curing, pickling, drying, and storage; and planning meals with limited supplies and field conditions.

Cooking supports the creation of food-based items, rations, and restorative meals. It focuses on nutrition, safety, and quality rather than alchemical transformation or weaponized toxins.

perks.name: Camp Kitchen
perks.description: You have the Cook's Utensils and can produce reliable meals from field supplies.

benefits.title: Recipe access
benefits.description: Unlocks cooking recipe tiers 0 to 1.

benefits.title: Steady prep
benefits.description: +1 to crafting roll quality via reduced effective DC.

perks.name: Provisioner
perks.description: You can stretch supplies and preserve nutrition without sacrificing consistency.

benefits.title: Recipe access
benefits.description: Unlocks cooking recipe tiers 2 to 7.

benefits.title: Ingredient recovery
benefits.description: On success, keep half the ingredients.

perks.name: Feastwright
perks.description: Your timing and heat control let you produce richer, complex dishes at scale.

benefits.title: Recipe access
benefits.description: Unlocks cooking recipe tiers 8 to 14.

benefits.title: Kitchen discipline
benefits.description: On failed cooking attempts, lose only half the ingredients.

perks.name: Master Culinarian
perks.description: You can run a kitchen under pressure and consistently execute top-tier culinary preparations.

benefits.title: Recipe access
benefits.description: Unlocks cooking recipe tiers 15 to 20.

benefits.title: Master precision
benefits.description: -1 crafting DC when crafting within unlocked tier.

perks.name: Improvised Pantry
perks.description: You can improvise from incomplete supplies and still produce edible, useful outcomes.

benefits.title: Experimental cooking
benefits.description: May attempt cooking recipes above your unlocked tier.

benefits.title: Experimental wildcard
benefits.description: Experimental attempts include one wrong ingredient to identify and remove.

benefits.title: Adaptive palate
benefits.description: +2 to experimental crafting roll total.

perks.name: Savvy Shopper
perks.description: You know how to read markets, negotiate fairly, and secure better ingredients for less.

benefits.title: Market leverage
benefits.description: +2 to persuasion checks when shopping/bartering.

benefits.title: Frugal sourcing
benefits.description: On success, keep half the ingredients.

perks.name: Fast Prep
perks.description: You can stage and execute cooking workflows quickly without sacrificing consistency.

benefits.title: Compressed workflow
benefits.description: Reduce required cooking time by 25%.

benefits.title: Clean execution
benefits.description: -1 crafting DC when crafting within unlocked tier.

perks.name: Service Rush
perks.description: You thrive in high-pressure service windows and can convert perfect timing into extra output.

benefits.title: Critical service
benefits.description: Nat 20 auto-succeeds with double output; nat 1 auto-fails with self-damage.

benefits.title: Waste reduction
benefits.description: On failed cooking attempts, lose only half the ingredients.

skills.name: Healing
skills.description:
Healing is the disciplined craft of stabilizing the wounded, restoring vitality, and treating injury and illness through practical medical knowledge. It governs the use of bandages, poultices, tonics, salves, and field techniques to preserve life and support recovery.

It encompasses three primary areas:

  • Assessment & Stabilization: Identifying wounds and conditions; stopping bleeding; setting fractures; cleaning injuries; preventing infection; and stabilizing a creature at the brink of death.
  • Restoration & Treatment: Applying poultices, salves, herbal remedies, and restorative draughts; treating poison exposure and disease symptoms; and accelerating natural recovery through proper care.
  • Field Medicine & Recovery: Managing limited supplies; tending multiple patients under pressure; preparing travel-ready medical kits; and overseeing rest and convalescence in harsh environments.

Healing supports the creation and use of restorative items, medical supplies, and recovery-focused consumables. It emphasizes preservation of life and physical recovery rather than alchemical experimentation or magical spellcasting.

perks.name: Basic Care
perks.description: You possess a Healer's Kit and the knowledge to use it properly. You can stabilize wounds, clean injuries, and provide competent field treatment.

skills.name: Smithing
skills.description:
Smithing is the craft of shaping, forging, and refining metal into functional forms. It governs heating, hammering, tempering, joining, and finishing metal components for tools, hardware, and structural use.

It encompasses three primary areas:

  • Metal Preparation: Assessing raw metal; removing impurities; managing heat; and preparing material for shaping without compromising integrity.
  • Forging & Forming: Hammering, bending, drawing, riveting, and welding metal into precise shapes; creating balanced and durable components; and maintaining structural strength during shaping.
  • Tempering & Finish: Hardening and tempering metal for resilience; refining edges and surfaces; reinforcing stress points; and applying protective finishes.

Smithing supports the creation of metal fittings, structural components, tools, hardware, and forged elements used in broader crafting. It focuses on material strength and precision shaping rather than enchantment or alchemical transformation.

perks.name: Basic Smithing
perks.description: You have Smith's Tools and the foundational knowledge to heat, shape, and temper metal safely. You can produce reliable metal components, fittings, and basic forged items suitable for practical use.

skills.name: Leatherworking
skills.description:
Leatherworking is the craft of transforming hides, pelts, and treated skins into durable, flexible materials and finished goods. It governs curing, cutting, stitching, shaping, and reinforcing leather for practical use in equipment, clothing, and crafted components.

It encompasses three primary areas:

  • Curing & Treatment: Cleaning raw hides; tanning and preserving material; preventing rot and brittleness; and preparing leather for long-term durability.
  • Cutting & Construction: Measuring, trimming, stitching, riveting, and layering leather into functional forms such as straps, sheaths, pouches, and fitted pieces.
  • Reinforcement & Finish: Strengthening stress points; adding linings or structural supports; sealing against moisture; and finishing surfaces for protection or aesthetic appeal.

Leatherworking supports the creation of gear, bindings, cases, flexible armor components, and fitted coverings. It focuses on structural integrity and material performance rather than metal forging or textile weaving.

perks.name: Basic Leatherworking
perks.description: You have Leatherworker's Tools and the foundational knowledge to cure hides, cut clean patterns, and stitch durable leather goods. You can produce functional straps, sheaths, pouches, and fitted components suitable for everyday use.

skills.name: Tinkering
skills.description:
Tinkering is the craft of designing, assembling, and repairing small mechanical devices and precision components. It governs gears, springs, housings, simple mechanisms, and the careful integration of moving parts into reliable constructs.

It encompasses three primary areas:

  • Mechanical Assembly: Constructing and aligning gears, levers, pins, and springs; ensuring balanced motion; and minimizing friction and wear.
  • Repair & Adjustment: Diagnosing mechanical faults; restoring damaged components; recalibrating misaligned parts; and improving reliability through fine adjustment.
  • Integration & Innovation: Combining mechanical elements with crafted materials; building compact mechanisms; and designing functional devices within practical size constraints.

Tinkering supports the creation of tools, small devices, precision mechanisms, and integrated components used in broader crafting systems. It focuses on mechanical reliability and functional design rather than large-scale construction or magical binding.

perks.name: Basic Tinkering
perks.description: You have Tinker's Tools and the foundational understanding to assemble, repair, and adjust small mechanical devices. You can produce simple mechanisms and maintain precision components with dependable accuracy.

skills.name: Cartography
skills.description:
Cartography is the craft of measuring, recording, and communicating places through maps. It governs navigation-ready layouts, terrain interpretation, distance estimation, and the practical translation of a real location into something others can trust.

It encompasses three primary areas:

  • Survey & Observation: Reading terrain, landmarks, and routes; estimating scale and distance; and capturing accurate notes under field conditions.
  • Drafting & Representation: Building clear map structure with symbols, labels, legends, and consistent scale; producing usable diagrams for travel, exploration, and planning.
  • Navigation & Reliability: Correcting maps over time, cross-checking sources, and spotting errors or misleading information before it gets someone lost.

Cartography supports the creation of maps, charts, route guides, and location records. It focuses on clarity and accuracy rather than artistic illustration.

perks.name: Basic Cartography
perks.description: You have Cartographer's Tools and the instincts to turn travel into something legible. You can sketch reliable maps, mark routes, and capture landmarks with a consistent scale and clear legend.

skills.name: Inscription
skills.description:
Inscription is the disciplined craft of marking language, symbols, and structured meaning onto physical surfaces. It governs precise writing, rune carving, sigil placement, and the durable recording of knowledge for practical or arcane use.

It encompasses three primary areas:

  • Script & Precision: Producing clean, legible text across materials such as parchment, wood, metal, and stone; maintaining alignment, spacing, and clarity under controlled or field conditions.
  • Symbol & Rune Craft: Accurately reproducing sigils, seals, glyphs, and structured markings that must retain exact form to function as intended.
  • Material Application: Selecting appropriate tools, inks, etching methods, or carving techniques to ensure markings endure without distortion or degradation.

Inscription supports the creation of scrolls, engraved plates, runic objects, and structured written records. It focuses on accuracy, durability, and symbolic integrity rather than artistic illustration or magical synthesis.

perks.name: Basic Inscription
perks.description: You possess Calligrapher's Supplies and the steady hand required to record language and symbols with clarity. You can produce clean script, consistent markings, and durable written records suitable for study, labeling, or formal documentation.

skills.name: Enchanting
skills.description:
Enchanting is the structured craft of binding magical properties to objects through deliberate ritual, symbolic alignment, and controlled infusion. It governs the attachment, stabilization, and refinement of arcane effects within physical forms.

It encompasses three primary areas:

  • Arcane Imbuement: Channeling magical energy into prepared objects; anchoring effects to physical forms; and ensuring compatibility between material and intended enchantment.
  • Stabilization & Control: Preventing magical backlash, energy bleed, or structural failure; reinforcing bindings; and managing volatility during infusion.
  • Runic & Symbolic Structure: Aligning glyphs, sigils, and symbolic frameworks that allow magical effects to persist in a controlled and repeatable manner.

Enchanting supports the creation and enhancement of magical items, infused tools, and stabilized arcane constructs. It focuses on controlled magical binding rather than raw spellcasting or alchemical reaction.

perks.name: Basic Enchanting
perks.description: You have access to Enchanter's Tools and the foundational knowledge to bind minor magical properties to prepared objects. You can safely perform basic infusions and stabilize simple enchantments.

skills.name: Gemcraft
skills.description:
Gemcraft is the disciplined craft of cutting, shaping, and setting gemstones for structural, aesthetic, and arcane purposes. It governs precision cutting, flaw assessment, polishing, and the preparation of gems for use in crafting, enchantment, or trade.

It encompasses three primary areas:

  • Assessment & Grading: Identifying gem types; recognizing flaws, fractures, and inclusions; determining quality and value; and judging suitability for structural or arcane use.
  • Cutting & Shaping: Splitting, faceting, grinding, and polishing stones to maximize clarity, strength, and energy alignment.
  • Setting & Stabilization: Mounting gems securely into metal, wood, or other materials; ensuring structural integrity; and preparing stones for magical infusion or mechanical integration.

Gemcraft supports the refinement of gemstones for jewelry, embedded components, arcane focuses, and precision fittings. It focuses on structural precision and material integrity rather than magical binding or mineral extraction.

perks.name: Basic Gemcraft
perks.description: You have Jeweler's Tools and the steady precision required to cut, polish, and set gemstones. You can assess quality, shape stones safely, and prepare gems for mounting or crafting.

skills.name: Tailoring
skills.description:
Tailoring is the craft of cutting, shaping, and assembling textiles into functional and fitted garments. It governs pattern drafting, stitching, reinforcement, and the transformation of cloth and soft materials into durable clothing and fabric components.

It encompasses three primary areas:

  • Pattern & Measurement: Drafting accurate patterns; measuring for proper fit; planning seams, folds, and structure; and minimizing material waste.
  • Construction & Stitching: Cutting fabric cleanly; hand-sewing or machine stitching; reinforcing stress points; and assembling layered or lined garments.
  • Finish & Reinforcement: Hemming, sealing seams, adding closures or fastenings, and ensuring garments maintain shape and durability under regular use.

Tailoring supports the creation of clothing, padded armor layers, fabric linings, banners, and flexible textile components. It focuses on fit, structure, and material integrity rather than leather curing or metal fabrication.

perks.name: Basic Tailoring
perks.description: You have Weaver's Tools and the foundational skill to measure, cut, and stitch textiles into reliable garments and fabric components. You can produce fitted clothing, simple padded layers, and reinforced cloth items suitable for everyday use.

skills.name: Masonry
skills.description:
Masonry is the craft of shaping, fitting, and assembling stone for structural and decorative purposes. It governs quarrying, cutting, carving, and joining stone to produce durable constructions and precise stone components.

It encompasses three primary areas:

  • Stone Selection & Assessment: Identifying suitable stone types; recognizing fractures and weaknesses; judging structural integrity; and selecting material appropriate to load, environment, and purpose.
  • Cutting & Shaping: Splitting, chiseling, smoothing, and carving stone to precise dimensions; creating interlocking forms; and producing fitted blocks, panels, and structural elements.
  • Assembly & Reinforcement: Laying stone with proper alignment and support; applying mortar or dry-fit techniques; reinforcing stress points; and ensuring long-term stability.

Masonry supports the construction of walls, foundations, statues, structural components, and fitted stonework. It focuses on durability, precision, and load-bearing integrity rather than mineral extraction or metal forging.

perks.name: Basic Masonry
perks.description: You have Mason's Tools and the foundational knowledge to cut, shape, and set stone with reliable precision. You can produce fitted blocks, simple carvings, and structurally sound stonework suitable for practical construction.
